$schema: Quest complication presets. Used by 'Add complication' dropdown in QuestEditor and as seed library for the AI complication generator.
$note: Categories organize the picker UI. ai_hint guides AI when expanding a preset into a quest-specific complication. Add freely — slugs are stable keys.

categories.label: False premise
categories.description: The quest's obvious story is wrong

categories.label: Escalating stakes
categories.description: Things get worse along the way

categories.label: External pressure
categories.description: Time, law, or nature forces a hand

complications.label: Quest-giver lies
complications.description: The one who gave the quest has hidden or twisted the truth about the motive or goal.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest seems too straightforward — reveal that the obvious story isn't the whole picture.
complications.ai_hint: The specific lie the quest-giver told, and what the truth actually is. Why did they lie?

complications.label: The victim doesn't want rescuing
complications.description: The person the party must rescue has chosen their situation — or is better off where they are.
complications.when_to_use: Rescue quests that need a twist. Forces players to consider what 'rescue' really means.
complications.ai_hint: Why doesn't the victim want rescuing? Three concrete reasons that make the choice hard.

complications.label: The target is already dead
complications.description: The person the party was to rescue, or the object they were to find, is already lost by the time they arrive.
complications.when_to_use: When you want to shift focus from 'can we make it?' to 'what do we do now?'
complications.ai_hint: How did the target die or vanish? What traces remain? What is now at stake?

complications.label: The reward doesn't exist
complications.description: The promised payment, treasure, or relic doesn't exist — or is already gone.
complications.when_to_use: Quests where the party chases pure gold. Forces a conversation about why they still do the right thing.
complications.ai_hint: What does the party discover instead of the reward? Who knew from the start?

complications.label: The monster protects the area
complications.description: The 'evil' force is actually holding something worse at bay. Killing it triggers the catastrophe.
complications.when_to_use: A classic monster quest with a clear antagonist. Flip the whole premise.
complications.ai_hint: What is the antagonist holding back? What happens if the party wins conventionally?

complications.label: Allied NPC betrays the party
complications.description: An NPC the party trusts is working for the other side or has their own agenda.
complications.when_to_use: When an NPC has been 'too' helpful. Plant clues early so it feels fair.
complications.ai_hint: Which NPC betrays them, when does it happen, and what is the traitor's real motive?

complications.label: An innocent is framed as guilty
complications.description: The obvious suspect is innocent — the real culprit planted the evidence.
complications.when_to_use: Investigations and mysteries that need another layer.
complications.ai_hint: Who is the innocent? What false evidence points to them? Who planted it?

complications.label: Two factions want the same object
complications.description: The quest object is coveted by at least two parties — the party can't satisfy everyone.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest lacks social tension. Forces a choice about whom to befriend or anger.
complications.ai_hint: Which two factions? Why does each want the object? What does each one offer?

complications.label: A third party got there first
complications.description: When the party arrives, the target has already been taken by someone else — now they must track it.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest is becoming predictable. Switch from a direct assault to a chase.
complications.ai_hint: Who took it? Where are they now? What traces did they leave?

complications.label: The party is being followed
complications.description: Another party shadows the group — a spy, assassin, or rival waiting for their chance.
complications.when_to_use: Mid-quest when tension flattens. Hint subtly a couple of sessions before the reveal.
complications.ai_hint: Who is following? What do they want? When do they strike, and how?

complications.label: The solution creates a bigger problem
complications.description: Completing the quest correctly triggers a larger crisis — short term wins, long term loses.
complications.when_to_use: Sets up a longer arc. Let the party win, but plant the seed of the next storyline.
complications.ai_hint: What larger crisis arises as a direct consequence of solving the quest?

complications.label: The culprit was coerced
complications.description: The antagonist did the wrong thing under duress — blackmail, magic, or to protect someone.
complications.when_to_use: When a clearly 'evil' figure needs nuance. Forces a choice between punishment and understanding.
complications.ai_hint: Who coerced the antagonist? How? What would they lose by refusing?

complications.label: Local custom forbids the obvious solution
complications.description: The easiest solution breaks a local tradition, law, or taboo — one the locals will not forgive.
complications.when_to_use: When cultural worldbuilding should come forward. Forces creative problem-solving.
complications.ai_hint: Which custom? What are the consequences of breaking it? Is there an accepted alternative?

complications.label: A PC becomes the target
complications.description: The antagonist shifts focus — suddenly a specific party member is under direct threat.
complications.when_to_use: When a player has been quiet. Pulls them into the spotlight.
complications.ai_hint: Which PC? Why them specifically? What is the threat, and how visible is it?

complications.label: The antagonist has a personal tie
complications.description: The antagonist is a PC's old friend, family member, mentor, or object of revenge.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest needs emotional weight. Arrange with the player beforehand for best effect.
complications.ai_hint: What tie? Does the PC know? What changes when the truth is revealed?

complications.label: The world changes mid-quest
complications.description: A natural disaster, political upheaval, or magical event changes the quest's frame.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest has dragged on. Forces the party to reinterpret their priorities.
complications.ai_hint: What change? How suddenly does it happen? How does it affect the quest goal?

complications.label: Time runs out
complications.description: A hard deadline is introduced or shortened — the party can't do everything they wanted.
complications.when_to_use: When players hesitate or over-optimize. Forces decisions.
complications.ai_hint: What is the deadline? What happens if it's missed? What must the party sacrifice to make it?

complications.label: Authorities pursue the party
complications.description: Guards, knights, or bounty hunters are after the party — often over a misunderstanding.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest lacks urgency. Add a layer of 'we're being hunted too'.
complications.ai_hint: Who is after them and why? Is it a misunderstanding or something they actually did?

complications.label: Quest-giver dies before payment
complications.description: The one who hired the party dies or vanishes — with no will, and relatives deny everything.
complications.when_to_use: When a quest was purely transactional. Forces a conversation about why the party does this at all.
complications.ai_hint: How does the quest-giver die? Who denies the agreement? Are there witnesses or documents?
